name: ritual-chamber-dark
summary: A chamber prepared for occult, necrotic, or infernal rites. Blood-grooves cut into the floor, irregular symbols, braziers that burn with wrong-colored flame. Charged and unstable.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: square or circular — the circle still anchors the room, but its character is inverted

accent.purpose: the sigil — grooves stained or inlaid differently

primary_palette.note: summoning sigil with braziers at cardinal points
dark altar amid piled remains of prior rites
a skull-warded circle — offerings at each quarter
a black monolith that the chamber is built around; rituals performed against its face

story_prompts: a summoning complete — the circle's occupant has left; the summoners did not
fresh blood in the grooves — the rite was interrupted minutes ago
a generational chamber — layer upon layer of sigil scarring, newest one not yet finished
a single cultist rehearsing — chalked annotations in the margins, a manual propped open
the rite misfired — char-marks radiating outward, the altar cracked, a scorched shape on the wall

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: low and wrong (0.15-0.25) — green, blue, or red-tinged ambient; braziers the dominant source

anti_patterns: symmetry should feel WRONG — slightly off-axis sigils, unequal counts
no pews or worship seating — this is practitioner space, not congregant
avoid bright warm lighting — the mood is sickly, cold, or infernal
